title: Workbuddy Agent (Batch 3 — issue #204)
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter of .github/workbuddy/agents/*.md. The Markdown body below the closing `---` IS the prompt template (rendered as a Go text/template against runtime.TaskContext). Frontmatter no longer accepts `prompt:`. As of batch 3, label-flip instructions (the `gh issue edit` command and the available `status:*` outcomes) are NOT authored in the prompt body — the runtime injects them automatically as a transition footer derived from the workflow state's transitions map. Linter codes WB-L001/WB-L002 fire when the body still inlines `gh issue edit` or a `status:` literal. Keep this in sync with internal/config/types.go (AgentConfig).
$comment: Batch 3: transition footer is runtime-generated from the workflow state's transitions map and appended to the prompt body at dispatch. The prompt body should describe the agent's job in prose; do not embed the gh issue edit command or status:* labels manually.

name.description: Stable identifier referenced by workflow states. Should match the file basename stem.

description.description: Human-readable summary, displayed in `workbuddy status` and similar surfaces.

runner.description: Where the runtime executes. Defaults to local.

state.description: Workflow state name (NOT a label) that hands work to this agent. Validator code WB-X007 fires when no workflow declares this state.

event.description: Event currently always `labeled`. Future batch may broaden the set.

context.description: TaskContext field paths the prompt body references (e.g. `Repo`, `Issue.Number`, `Issue.Comments[].Author`). Required: every dotted path used in the body must appear here. Validator codes WB-CT001 (missing/empty), WB-CT002 (used but not declared), WB-CT003 (declared but unused).

command.description: Legacy command-template field; alternative to a body-as-prompt agent. Kept for back-compat with command-mode agents.

timeout.description: Go duration string. Should be >= worker.stale_inference.idle_threshold to avoid watchdog kills (validator code WB-S001).

permissions.description: Subprocess capability boundaries. See internal/config/types.go PermissionsConfig for the full structure.

schema_file.description: Path (relative to the agent file) of a JSON schema describing the structured output.

timeout.description: Deprecated alias of policy.timeout, kept for back-compat.
